creator: mel@rachie.is-a-geek.net
text:
On Monday 23 March 2009 12:02:17 Pav Lucistnik wrote:
> Mel p=C3=AD=C5=A1e v po 23. 03. 2009 v 11:35 +0100:
> > On Sunday 22 March 2009 11:32:29 pav@freebsd.org wrote:
> > > State-Changed-Why:
> > > Similar but different patch committed
> >
> > Thanks! If you like, I can submit a list of origins or a patch against
> > their respective makefiles for ports I know to be unsafe. Some (f.e.
> > audio/libsndfile) are caused by autotools, as they want to run aclocal =
in
> > parallel. Maybe this can be fixed more generically. Currently the list
> > has 22 ports out of approx 750 origins made.
>
> Well sure, send them in. But I'm still unsure how to approach this --
> currently it's whitelist only (and no entries in the whitelist as of
> now, yet). But I think I could put your list into wiki for now.

=46or a number of ports it's unimportant if built with -j or not as they ha=
ve=20
only a few or even one (sysutils/pstree f.e.) source files. This could serv=
e=20
as a priority list: if a port has >X source files, then ping MAINTAINER to=
=20
test job safeness. The package building servers' logs could serve as input =
for=20
this, by checking the number of lines in the build target, and compensating=
=20
for the libtool annoyance of printing 2 lines for one compilation.

The unsafe list is:
% grep -B1 WANT_MAKE_COMPAT /etc/make.conf|sed -n 's,^.*\.CURDIR:M\*/\(.*\)=
),
\1,p'|sort
audio/nas
devel/gperf
devel/nasm
devel/pth
editors/vim
graphics/libart_lgpl
lang/lua50
lang/ocaml
net-mgmt/net-snmp
security/libgpg-error
www/lynx

This list works if -B is used: targets need to be made in order, possibly t=
he=20
root Makefile of this list can be patched to properly set dependencies.

% grep -B1 NO_PARALLEL /etc/make.conf|sed -n 's,^.*\.CURDIR:M\*/\(.*\)),\1,=
p'|
sort
audio/xfce4-mixer
devel/dbus-qt4
devel/doxygen
devel/libthai
games/falconseye
misc/e2fsprogs-libuuid
print/ghostscript8
security/nss
textproc/xerces-c2
x11-clocks/kdetoys3

=2DB doesn't work or not tested as error was not directly make related: i.e=
=2E=20
port ran aclocal/autoconf once build started.
=2D-=20
Mel
